title: Return Values
description: Send a computed value back to the code that called the function.

slides.title: Returning Data 🔙
slides.content:
The `void` keyword used in previous examples indicates that the function should not return a value.

If you want the function to compute something and return an answer, you must configure its **return type** instead of void (e.g. `int`, `string`, etc).

slides.title: A Math Function
slides.content: Notice how we replaced `void` with `int`, and used the `return` keyword.

quizData.question: What is the return type of the addFive function?
quizData.explanation: The function evaluates to an `int` (integer) because it declares 'int addFive(int x)'.

slides.title: Storing Returns 📦
slides.content: Returned numbers aren't just for printing! You can store a function's return value in a variable.

slides.title: Example

quizData.question: A function that has a return type of `string` MUST return a piece of text.
quizData.explanation: If you promise the compiler you will return a string (e.g. string getPassword()), you MUST use a return statement returning a text string.
